zh.Farringdon, Devon, England: 英國英格蘭戴文法林格頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Charles Farringdon於大約1499年出生在英國戴文時，他的父親John Farringdon II31歲，母親Elizabeth Wilford42歲。他和Margery Stucley至少有2個兒子和4個女兒。他去世在英國。

zh-hans.Farringdon, Devon, England: 英国英格兰戴文法林格頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Charles Farringdon于大约1499年出生在英国戴文时，他的父亲John Farringdon II31岁，母亲Elizabeth Wilford42岁。他和Margery Stucley至少有2个儿子和4个女儿。他去世在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Farringdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Farndon
meanings: English: locative name from various places named with Old English fearn ‘fern, bracken’ + dūn ‘hill’, such as East Farndon (Northants), West Farndon in Woodford (Northants), Farndon (Cheshire, Notts), Faringdon (Berks, Dorset, Hants), Little Faringdon (Oxon), Farringdon (Devon), Farringdon in North Petherton (Somerset), and Farringdon Hill in Stogursey (Somerset), all of which share similar medieval spellings. This surname may have become confused with Farrington .

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Charles Farringdon was born about 1499, in Devon, England, his father, John Farringdon II, was 31 and his mother, Elizabeth Wilford, was 42. He had at least 2 sons and 4 daughters with Margery Stucley. He died in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Charles Farringdon est né environ 1499, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, John Farringdon II, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Wilford, avait 42 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles avec Margery Stucley. Il est décédé à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Charles Farringdon um 1499 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Farringdon II 31 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Wilford 42. Er hatte mit Margery Stucley mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Charles Farringdon è nato il circa 1499 nel Devon, England, il suo padre, John Farringdon II, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Wilford, aveva 42 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie con Margery Stucley. Morì nel England.

ja.Farringdon, Devon, England: イギリスイギリスデボンファリングドン
ja.Farrington, Dorset, England: イギリスドールセトファリントン
ja.lifeSummary: Charles Farringdonが約1499年にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父John Farringdon IIは31歳，母Elizabeth Wilfordは42歳であった。彼はMargery Stucleyとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼はイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Charles Farringdon(이)가 대략 1499년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Farringdon II(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Wilford(은)는 42세였다. 그는 Margery Stucley(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국에서 사망했다.

mn.Farringdon, Devon, England: Farringdon, Devon, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Charles Farringdon nasceu aproximadamente 1499, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Farringdon II, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Wilford, tinha 42 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas com Margery Stucley. Ele faleceu em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Charles Farringdon родился около 1499 г. в местности девон, Англия, его отцу по имени John Farringdon II было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Wilford, 42. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери от Margery Stucley. Он умер в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Charles Farringdon nació aproximadamente 1499, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, John Farringdon II, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Wilford, tenía 42 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas con Margery Stucley. Murió en Inglaterra.
